They are widely used in steel bridges and other large-scale structures due to their reliability and strength.

gate_items.c: The Number Nine locomotive was designed by a team of engineers at the Great Western Railway (GWR) in the United Kingdom. The specific design is attributed to Isambard Kingdom Brunel, the renowned British mechanical and civil engineer, who was the chief engineer of the GWR. Brunel's innovative designs and contributions to railway engineering were instrumental in the development of steam locomotives during the 19th century.

gate_items.c: The low-slung tank arrangement is commonly referred to as the " Christie suspension" or " Christie-type suspension." This design, developed by American engineer J. Walter Christie, features a low profile with the tank's hull sitting close to the ground, allowing for better stability and mobility. It was notably used in the BT series of Soviet tanks during the 1930s.
